mapping.a_element: cyclic, tumbling motion of individual polymer chains in a dense brush under shear
mapping.b_element: tumbling motion of a single polymer molecule in shear flow
strong deviation of probability distribution functions (PDF) of polymer orientation angles from Gaussian

mapping.a_element: Couette and Poiseuille flow as shear environments
mapping.b_element: shear flow with thermal noise as the experimental environment

mapping.a_element: control via shear rate (implicit in flow conditions)
inversion of hydrodynamic flow direction near the brush-coated surface
mapping.b_element: no direct counterpart (collective effect absent in single-molecule study)

brief_justification: Both papers study the tumbling dynamics of polymer chains under shear, with a focus on non-Gaussian orientation statistics and scaling with shear rate (or Wi). The core mechanism—a polymer chain undergoing cyclic reorientation in shear flow—is identical, though paper A extends this to collective effects in a brush, while paper B focuses on single-molecule statistics.
